description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must express “environment hardening” as code.  
  Use the terminal to turn the following policy into an automated, self-documenting implementation.

  POLICY TO ENFORCE  
    1. Every interactive shell must have  
         DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1  
         TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true  
         AWS_REGION=us-east-1  
    2. The policy itself must live under a dedicated folder that can be version-controlled.  
    3. A log proving that the policy was applied has to be produced in a very specific, testable format.

  REQUIRED END-STATE (what the automated grader will look for)

  A. Directory hierarchy  
     /home/user/policy-as-code                (must exist)  
     /home/user/policy-as-code/policies       (must exist)

  B. Policy definition file  
     Path  : /home/user/policy-as-code/policies/env_policy.yaml  
     Format: exactly the three lines shown (YAML, 2-space indent, Unix line-endings)  

       required_env:
         - DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST
         - TF_IN_AUTOMATION
         - AWS_REGION

  C. Enforcement script  
     Path        : /home/user/policy-as-code/policies/apply_policy.sh  
     Must be     : executable (chmod +x)  
     Must start  : with the she-bang “#!/usr/bin/env bash” on line 1  
     Must contain: one export per variable, with the precise values below—quotation marks optional but keys and values
                   are case-sensitive and must appear exactly once each in the file.  

       export DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1
       export TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true
       export AWS_REGION=us-east-1

     The script, when run, must also:  
       • append the same three export commands to /home/user/.bashrc (if they are not present already)  
       • echo the three variables in KEY=VALUE form to STDOUT (one per line, in the order shown above)  
       • write the compliance log described in section D.

  D. Compliance log  
     Path   : /home/user/policy-as-code/compliance.log  
     Format : exactly three lines, Unix line-endings. Each line is:  
              <KEY>=<VALUE> - enforced  
              Example of a correct first line:  
              DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1 - enforced  
     Order  : the lines MUST appear in the order:  
              DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST, then TF_IN_AUTOMATION, then AWS_REGION

  E. Permanent shell configuration  
     /home/user/.bashrc must now contain the three export statements.  The grader will search for the literal strings  
       export DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1  
       export TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true  
       export AWS_REGION=us-east-1  

  HOW YOU WILL BE GRADED  
    • Presence and exact contents of the files/directories listed above.  
    • apply_policy.sh has executable permission and the required lines.  
    • /home/user/.bashrc contains the export lines.  
    • compliance.log exists and matches the required 3-line format exactly.

  You should need no more than ~5–10 shell commands to accomplish everything (mkdir, echo/cat, chmod, etc.).  
  Do NOT place any additional files outside /home/user/policy-as-code.  
  Do NOT hardcode absolute paths other than those specified.  

  When you are finished, the policy folder will be self-contained, reproducible, and immediately usable by another engineer who only needs to run policies/apply_policy.sh.
truth:
Expected structure and contents after correct completion:

  1. Directories
     - /home/user/policy-as-code                  (exists, type=dir)
     - /home/user/policy-as-code/policies         (exists, type=dir)

  2. File: /home/user/policy-as-code/policies/env_policy.yaml  
     Exact 4-line content (including trailing newline after the last line):
       required_env:
         - DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST
         - TF_IN_AUTOMATION
         - AWS_REGION

  3. File: /home/user/policy-as-code/policies/apply_policy.sh  
     • Must be executable (mode 0755 is acceptable).  
     • Must begin with: #!/usr/bin/env bash (line 1).  
     • Within the file an automated grep ‑Eq '^export DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1$' must succeed.  
       Same for the other two export lines.  
     • The script may contain additional logic but those three export lines must appear exactly once each.

  4. File: /home/user/policy-as-code/compliance.log  
     Exact 3-line content (each followed by '\n'):
       DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1 - enforced
       TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true - enforced
       AWS_REGION=us-east-1 - enforced

  5. File: /home/user/.bashrc  
     Must contain the three export commands on any lines (order does not matter for validation here)
     but each must appear literally:
       export DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1
       export TF_IN_AUTOMATION=true
       export AWS_REGION=us-east-1
